task: Close out Epic 06 and activate Oracle data-plane epic
acceptance_criteria: Post-Epic-06 review is explicit and the full Oracle data-plane epic skeleton exists
validation_command: bash -lc \grep -q 'Oracle Data Plane and Minimum Insights' EPIC_ROADMAP.md && test -f .codex-tasks/20260420-oracle-data-plane-epic/EPIC.md && test -f .codex-tasks/20260420-oracle-data-plane-epic/SUBTASKS.csv && test -f .codex-tasks/20260420-oracle-data-plane-epic/PROGRESS.md\""
notes: Roadmap 01-06 is exhausted, so the new active epic was created from explicit Oracle validation-only gaps before any product code changed

task: Freeze engine-aware metric sample and ClickHouse schema baseline
acceptance_criteria: Metric samples and ClickHouse schema carry explicit engine-aware telemetry semantics without faking Oracle collection support
notes: MetricSample now carries engine identity, ClickHouse schema v2 uses the generic `metric_samples` table with an explicit engine column, and targeted schema/pipeline/analytics regressions stayed green

task: Implement Oracle collector and worker dispatch baseline
acceptance_criteria: Validated Oracle instances can enter a real collection loop and write the approved minimal Oracle metrics
validation_command: uv run pytest tests/scheduler tests/worker_mysql tests/worker_oracle tests/integration/metrics_pipeline && pnpm test:control-plane:oracle
notes: OracleMetricsCollector, OracleMetricsWorker, and EngineAwareMetricsWorker now close the scheduler -> queue -> worker loop; offline suites and the live Oracle control-plane gate both passed

task: Expose minimal Oracle analytics API contract
acceptance_criteria: Analytics routes and typed contract can read the approved Oracle metrics without pretending to offer full parity
validation_command: uv run pytest tests/api/analytics tests/integration/analytics_queries && pnpm openapi:check
notes: Instance trends are now engine-aware via `/analytics/instances/{instance_id}/trends`, Oracle detail metrics are covered by API and integration tests, and the MySQL route alias remains available off-schema for compatibility

task: Expose Oracle trends in the web detail flow
acceptance_criteria: The web detail surface can show Oracle trend data and updated capability semantics without regressing MySQL UX
validation_command: pnpm --filter web test && pnpm --filter web typecheck && pnpm --filter web build
notes: Oracle detail pages now fetch and render the generic trends contract, capability copy is truthful about the remaining boundary, and preview/test fixtures are aligned with the new minimal telemetry surface

acceptance_criteria: Repo-root Oracle data-plane signoff passes with backend, schema, live-gate, web, and smoke evidence
validation_command: pnpm openapi:check && uv run pytest tests/schema/test_schema_bootstrap.py tests/api/analytics tests/integration/metrics_pipeline tests/integration/analytics_queries tests/integration/control_plane && pnpm --filter web test && pnpm --filter web typecheck && pnpm --filter web build && pnpm smoke
notes: Root signoff command passed end-to-end, `pnpm test:control-plane:oracle` refreshed the live Oracle gate, and Epic 07 now closes with both MySQL/governance guardrails and second-engine data-plane evidence intact
